prospect.Summary: One of Eric's two sons in pro ball (Jared pitches in the Dodgers system), Kyle hit .276/.342/.407 at UCLA and signed for just over $430,000 in 2023. He hit .311/.390/.485 in his first full season, another of the Rockies' recent college draftees who put up monster numbers at hitter-friendly Spokane in 2024. Karros is very likely not that good a hitter. He's a stiff-legged athlete whose upright manner of swinging leaves him very vulnerable to secondary stuff at the bottom of the zone. Karros' carrying tool is his third base defense. He's not explosive or rangy, but he's incredibly smooth and capable of making great throws from all kinds of odd platforms. It's important for Karros to add strength to his 6-foot-5 frame, as he needs to develop power to counter a below-average contact projection. That and the introduction of other corner positions on defense are key developmental variables to watch as Karros treks toward a four corners bench role.

news.headline: Adds strength over offseason
news.news: Karros entered camp weighing 230 pounds, 20 pounds heavier than he was at the close of the 2025 season, Thomas Harding of MLB.com reports.
